papers.summary: Preference learning in Large Language Models (LLMs) has advanced significantly, yet existing methods remain limited by modest performance gains, high computational costs, hyperparameter sensitivity, and insufficient modeling of global token-level relationships. Candidates are organized as a tree: deeper trees accept more tokens per step, but adding depth requires sacrificing breadth (fallback options) under a fixed verification budget. primary_link.title: MTI: A Behavior-Based Temperament Profiling System for AI Agents

papers.summary:

radar.title_zh: 具備運行時安全監督的因果場景敘述，用於視覺-語言-動作駕駛

links.title: Ouroboros: Dynamic Weight Generation for Recursive Transformers via Input-Conditioned LoRA Modulation

primary_link.title: Ouroboros: Dynamic Weight Generation for Recursive Transformers via Input-Conditioned LoRA Modulation

top5.summary: Recursive transformers reuse a shared weight block across multiple depth steps, trading parameters for compute. A core limitation: every step applies the same transformation, preventing the model from composing distinct operations across depth. We present Ouroboros, a system that attaches a compact Controller hypernetwork to a recursive transformer block. The Controller observes the current hidden state, produces a per-step diagonal modulation vector, and applies it to frozen SVD-initialized LoR
top5.summary_zh: 遞迴型 Transformer 在多個深度步驟中重複使用共享權重區塊，以參數換取計算量。其核心限制是：每個步驟都應用相同的轉換，阻礙了模型在不同深度上組合不同的操作。我們提出 Ouroboros，一個將緊湊型 Controller hypernetwork 附加到遞迴型 Transformer 區塊的系統。該 Controller 觀察當前的隱藏狀態，生成一個逐步驟的對角調變向量，並將其應用於凍結的 SVD-initialized LoR。
top5.title: Ouroboros: Dynamic Weight Generation for Recursive Transformers via Input-Conditioned LoRA Modulation
top5.title_zh: Ouroboros: 透過輸入條件化的 LoRA 調變實現遞迴型 Transformer 的動態權重生成

links.title: SKILL0: In-Context Agentic Reinforcement Learning for Skill Internalization

primary_link.title: SKILL0: In-Context Agentic Reinforcement Learning for Skill Internalization

top5.summary: Agent skills, structured packages of procedural knowledge and executable resources that agents dynamically load at inference time, have become a reliable mechanism for augmenting LLM agents. Yet inference-time skill augmentation is fundamentally limited: retrieval noise introduces irrelevant guidance, injected skill content imposes substantial token overhead, and the model never truly acquires the knowledge it merely follows. We ask whether skills can instead be internalized into model parameter
top5.summary_zh: Agent skills，即代理在推理時動態載入的程式性知識和可執行資源的結構化套件，已成為增強 LLM agents 的可靠機制。然而，推理時的技能增強本質上是有限的：檢索噪音引入了不相關的引導，注入的技能內容造成了大量的 token 開銷，並且模型從未真正習得它僅僅遵循的知識。我們探討技能是否可以被內化到模型參數中
top5.title: SKILL0: In-Context Agentic Reinforcement Learning for Skill Internalization
top5.title_zh: SKILL0: 用於技能內化的 In-Context Agentic Reinforcement Learning

links.title: The Expert Strikes Back: Interpreting Mixture-of-Experts Language Models at Expert Level

primary_link.title: The Expert Strikes Back: Interpreting Mixture-of-Experts Language Models at Expert Level

top5.summary: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures have become the dominant choice for scaling Large Language Models (LLMs), activating only a subset of parameters per token. While MoE architectures are primarily adopted for computational efficiency, it remains an open question whether their sparsity makes them inherently easier to interpret than dense feed-forward networks (FFNs). We compare MoE experts and dense FFNs using $k$-sparse probing and find that expert neurons are consistently less polysemantic,
top5.summary_zh: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) 架構已成為擴展 Large Language Models (LLMs) 的主要選擇，每個 token 只激活一部分參數。雖然 MoE 架構主要因其計算效率而被採用，但其稀疏性是否使其本質上比 dense feed-forward networks (FFNs) 更容易解釋，仍然是一個懸而未決的問題。我們使用 $k$-sparse probing 比較了 MoE experts 和 dense FFNs，發現 expert neurons 始終較少多義性，
top5.title: The Expert Strikes Back: Interpreting Mixture-of-Experts Language Models at Expert Level
